---
title: SingleR annotation of tumour cells (full Aldinger reference)
date: 2023-06-01
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

[Cont'd from 2023-05-18]

To ensure SingleR is annotating the MB dataset correctly by using the full Aldinger dataset as a reference. The full dataset contains irrelevant cells (like Purkinje cells which should show a low score for all tumour cells) as well as additional cell types (like microglia, of which there is a cluster in the tumour samples).


## Files and directories

### Code

See `<tumour Vladoiu software directory>/annot_cluster_singleR.R`.

### Output

Output directory is at `<tumour Vladoiu results directory>/20230601`. The bash script in that directory was used to generate the output which is contained in two directories

<!-- &#x202F; is a narrow no-break space -->

The `all_cells` directory contains the results from using the full Aldinger dataset as a reference (69&#x202F;174 cells from 01k, 05k, 10k, 80k).

The `only_80k` directory contains the results from using only the 80k dataset (59&#x202F;608 cells) as a reference. I isolated this dataset since the Hendrikse RL-derived dataset only contains the cells from 80k.


## Results

::: {.callout-note}
Only the 80k results are shown here.
:::

![UMAP of tumour cells coloured by sample, cluster, and subtype. Figure from 2023-05-10.](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230510/mnn/umap.png){#fig-umap}


![Bar charts showing distribution of cells.](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230601/only_80k/annot_bar.png){#fig-bar-proportions}

Looking at @fig-bar-proportions, the SingleR predictions seem to be relatively similar to the results from 2023-05-18 (Group 3 is RL<sub>VZ</sub>/RL<sub>SVZ</sub>/Early UBC, Group 4 is Early UBC, SHH is GCP/GN). Interestingly, SingleR annotates some of the Group 3 and SHH tumour cells as "17-Brainstem" (middle row, first plot).

In the bottom row of @fig-bar-proportions, we can also clearly see two microglia clusters (10 and 18) as expected. Cluster 19 appears to contain a mix of various cell types; on the UMAP, this cluster is also separated from the others (@fig-umap, middle plot).


![SingleR score of each annotated tumour cell.](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230601/only_80k/score_heatmap.png){#fig-pred_score}

Clustering of the tumour cells' SingleR prediction scores clearly separates the SHH tumours from Groups 3 and 4. However, the Group 3 and Group 4 tumours are more mixed, although there does appear to be an inversely correlated spectrum of scores for the RL<sub>SVZ</sub> vs. Early UBCs (i.e., the tumour cells either have a high RL<sub>SVZ</sub> score or a high Early UBC score). The microglia are clearly separated from the tumour cells.

Next step is to re-run SingleR but with our conserved vs. human-specific UBC cell anonotation and see if the tumour cells are more similar to our human-specific cells.
